execution_overview.overall_summary: Executed Task1 (partial correlations and OLS with Age control) and Task2 (partial correlation with Age control) using Python rewrites without external libraries. Found strong negative associations between life history strategy (Mini-K, HKSS) and DSM-5 psychopathology controlling for Age. Both tasks produced usable JSON outputs.

deviations.description: Rewrote Task1 script to avoid non-standard Python libraries, implementing partial correlation and OLS with Age control using standard library only.
deviations.reason: Container raised ModuleNotFoundError for numpy/scipy during execution.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned partial correlations and OLS regressions; only implementation details were modified to ensure execution.

test_statistics.other: Key results: Partial r(MiniK, DSM5 | Age) = -0.5134, p = 4.35e-11, n = 138; OLS DSM5 ~ MiniK + Age: coef(MiniK) = -0.6418, p = 3.62e-12, beta_std = -0.4956. HKSS results: partial r(HKSS, DSM5 | Age) = -0.4088, p = 4.56e-07; OLS DSM5 ~ HKSS + Age: coef(HKSS) = -0.4141, p = 1.95e-07, beta_std = -0.3876.

result_raw.result_text: Mini-K and HKSS totals are negatively associated with DSM-5 psychopathology controlling for Age, both in partial correlations and OLS.

conclusion.conclusion_text: Results support the focal claim that life history strategy (higher Mini-K or HKSS, indicating slower strategy) is associated with lower DSM-5 psychopathology after controlling for Age.

deviations.description: Rewrote Task2 script to compute partial correlation using standard library only.
deviations.reason: Original script required numpy/scipy not available at runtime.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned partial correlation analysis with Age control using Mini-K and DSM-5 totals.

test_statistics.other: Degrees of freedom (partial corr with 1 control): 135

result_raw.result_text: Partial correlation between Mini-K and DSM-5 controlling for Age is negative and statistically significant.

conclusion.conclusion_text: The negative partial correlation supports the claim that higher life history strategy (Mini-K) relates to lower DSM-5 psychopathology when controlling for Age.
